codeReview.summary: The worker replaced the hand-coded IconBrainstorm in CustomIcons.tsx with a Gemini-generated version using a temporary generation script. The new icon is well-structured with brain hemispheres, center fissure, animated neural pathways (pathLength draw-in/reverse), pulsing synaptic spark circles, and radiating idea sparks. GeminiIcons.tsx was not modified. The icon follows the same patterns as all other icons in the file (SVGProps, motion from framer-motion, viewBox 0 0 24 24, currentColor, strokeWidth 1.5, className={props.className}). Build and lint pass cleanly. The worker used the explicitly described alternative approach from the feature description (temporary focused script) rather than modifying generate-icons.mjs, which was the correct decision since running the full script would have regenerated all 21 icons and changed GeminiIcons.tsx, violating the IMPORTANT constraint.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The generate-icons.mjs script contains a hardcoded Gemini API key (GEMINI_API_KEY = 'AIzaSyC3p64gzqUTuzeFDz7vqSiL3MIPafYTotw'). This is a pre-existing issue not introduced by this feature, but the worker's temporary script likely replicated this pattern. The knowledge base documents a 'hardcoded fallback API key' pattern as tech debt for backend endpoints, but does not mention the frontend scripts.

The icon generation pipeline has a limitation: running generate-icons.mjs regenerates ALL icons nondeterministically (Gemini output varies per call), making it impossible to add a single icon without changing others. The worker correctly identified this and used the alternative approach. This constraint could be documented in the knowledge base so future workers don't waste time trying the full regeneration path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker immediately understood the issue and created a focused single-icon generation script instead of modifying generate-icons.mjs. Feature description itself acknowledged this with the ALTERNATIVE approach.

summary: Clean pass. The worker replaced the hand-coded IconBrainstorm with a Gemini-generated version in CustomIcons.tsx. Only one file was modified. GeminiIcons.tsx is untouched. The new icon follows all existing patterns (SVGProps, framer-motion, currentColor, viewBox, className). Build and lint pass with no new errors. The worker chose the explicitly offered alternative approach (temporary focused script) over modifying generate-icons.mjs, which was the right call given the constraint that GeminiIcons.tsx must not change.
